lesson.title: Generics
lesson.summary: Write type-safe, reusable code with type parameters (Go 1.18+).

sections.heading: Type parameters
sections.bodyHtml:
Generics let you write functions and types that work with any type. A type parameter goes in square brackets:

func Last[T any](s []T) T {
    return s[len(s)-1]
}

fmt.Println(Last([]int{1, 2, 3}))        // 3
fmt.Println(Last([]string{"a", "b"}))    // "b"

The [T any] says "this function works for any type T". The compiler infers the type from the argument — you rarely need to specify it explicitly.

sections.heading: Type constraints
sections.bodyHtml:
Use constraints to restrict what types a parameter accepts. The constraints package provides common ones, or define your own:

import "golang.org/x/exp/constraints"

func Max[T constraints.Ordered](a, b T) T {
    if a > b {
        return a
    }
    return b
}

Any type that supports >, <, == satisfies Ordered (int, float64, string, etc.).

sections.heading: Generic types
sections.bodyHtml:
Types can also have type parameters. This is useful for collections:

type Stack[T any] struct {
    items []T
}

func (s *Stack[T]) Push(v T) {
    s.items = append(s.items, v)
}

func (s *Stack[T]) Pop() (T, bool) {
    if len(s.items) == 0 {
        var zero T
        return zero, false
    }
    v := s.items[len(s.items)-1]
    s.items = s.items[:len(s.items)-1]
    return v, true
}

Instantiate a generic type with concrete types: Stack[int]{}.

sections.heading: Your turn
sections.bodyHtml:
Open practice/generics/main.go.

Write a generic function Contains[T comparable](s []T, v T) bool that returns true if v is in the slice. Test it with int and string slices.

questions.prompt: Where do type parameters go in a generic function?

options.label: In parentheses before the function name
In square brackets between the function name and parameters
In angle brackets after the parameters

questions.prompt: What does the any constraint mean in [T any]?

options.label: T can be any type at all
T must be an integer type

questions.prompt: What constraint should you use for types that support comparison with ==?

exercises.title: Generic Contains
exercises.instructions: Write Contains[T comparable](s []T, v T) bool and test with int and string slices.
